proofBoundary: Export-proven workflow canvas layout guidance derived from two user-provided workflow JSON references. This reference validates node and sequence-flow layout readability only; it does not validate business semantics, assignee correctness, workflow execution, or runtime publishing.

sourceReferences.description: Complex approval workflow with horizontal approval backbone, inclusive branch gateways, lower-lane cancel/reject handling, and explicit vertex routing on many long transitions.
layoutPatterns: Use a readable horizontal backbone for the main happy path.
Place reject/cancel paths on separate vertical lanes instead of stacking them over task nodes.
Use vertices for long return, reject, merge, and cross-lane transitions.
Spread branch gateways and approval tasks across columns instead of reusing one coordinate.

sourceReferences.description: Large reimbursement workflow with multiple vertical lanes, recurring approval/action blocks, branch fan-out/fan-in, and rounded/vertex-routed sequence flows.
layoutPatterns: Use multiple vertical lanes for side branches, optional reviewers, finance handling, and final archival actions.
Use rounded sequence flows for complex routes.
Add vertices to long cross-lane links so lines do not cut through unrelated nodes.
Keep Start/End/reject outcomes visually separated from task/action clusters.

required: Every non-SequenceFlow node must carry a top-level position object with numeric x/y coordinates.
Generated nodes must not share identical or near-identical coordinates.
Complex workflows must reserve enough canvas width and height for the number of planned nodes.
Rejected, cancel, and exception paths must use a separate lane from the main approval path.
Rejected and cross-lane SequenceFlow edges must use rounded routing and explicit vertices where the source and target are far apart or on different lanes.
SequenceFlow source and target references must resolve to existing workflow node ids.
Generated graphposition should cover the visual node bounds plus padding.
